title: cheiranthus × cheiri var. corinthius
intro: Cheiranthus × cheiri var. corinthius, also known as the Cornish poppy or Shirley poppy, is a cultivated hybrid of the wild poppy (Papaver rhoeas) and the field poppy (Papaver argemone). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cheiranthus × cheiri var. corinthius include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce pain and inflammation, and act as a natural antacid. Cheiranthus × cheiri var. corinthius can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
